full_prompt: Design an Inconel 718 engine-mount thrust-link bracket in the spirit of a canonical engine-mount-bracket patent (US9796464B2-style), transferring forward thrust and side loads from a turbofan engine into the pylon primary structure. Envelope: 280 x 180 x 120 mm. Interfaces: engine-side spherical bearing pocket of 35 mm diameter (for a Corrotect-plated spherical bearing); pylon-side fastener grid of 8 bolts (NAS-1351 5/16 inch = 7.94 mm) on a 2x4 rectangular pattern with 40 mm transverse pitch and 50 mm longitudinal pitch; an additional redundant load-path interface (required by CS-25.571 fail-safe) with 2 secondary HI-LOK pins on the bracket body. Material: Inconel 718 per AMS 5663 (precipitation hardened, yield 1034 MPa, UTS 1275 MPa, Young's modulus 200 GPa, density 8190 kg/m^3, elongation 12%). Load cases (magnitudes flagged as placeholder pending patent fetch): LC1 forward thrust limit 45 kN along thrust axis; LC2 side load limit 15 kN (yaw gust); LC3 vertical (maneuver/gust) 12 kN; LC4 combined thrust + side 0.75x each (33.75 + 11.25 kN); LC5 ultimate 1.5 x LC1 = 67.5 kN; LC6 fail-safe case at 1.0x limit with primary load path cut (remaining redundant path must carry the load without catastrophic failure). Pass/fail per CS-25.571 + FAR 25.571 + CS-E engine integration: (R1) max von Mises at limit (LC1-4) <= yield = 1034 MPa (no permanent deformation); (R2) max von Mises at ultimate LC5 <= UTS = 1275 MPa; (R3) fail-safe LC6: with primary path cut, remaining structure max von Mises <= yield / 1.0 = 1034 MPa at limit, with a fail-safe SF 1.0 (inspect-before-next-flight criterion per CS-25.571 Amendment 18); (R4) max displacement at engine-side spherical bearing under LC1 <= 0.2 mm (thrust alignment requirement); (R5) fatigue life at 1 x 10^6 cycles of cyclic thrust (0 to 45 kN) must demonstrate a no-crack-growth damage-tolerance margin; this is tracked as a post-FEA closed-form damage-tolerance check (S-N curve, Inconel 718 HCF fatigue limit ~700 MPa), not detailed FEA; (R6) mass reduction vs solid-billet baseline >= 25% (optimization target typical for Inconel thrust-link parts). Deliverables: STEP AP242 single body; FEA report with per-case stress, fail-safe stress, and spherical-bearing deflection; fatigue summary referencing Inconel 718 S-N data; CS-25.571 compliance sheet showing fail-safe redundancy feature.
short_prompt:
Design an Inconel 718 engine-mount thrust-link bracket in the spirit of a canonical engine-mount-bracket patent (US9796464B2-style), transferring forward thrust and side loads from a turbofan engine into the pylon primary structure.

Envelope: 280 x 180 x 120 mm. Engine-side: spherical-bearing pocket 35 mm diameter (for a Corrotect-plated spherical bearing). Pylon-side: eight NAS-1351 5/16 inch (7.94 mm) bolts on a 2x4 grid with 40 mm transverse and 50 mm longitudinal pitch. Redundant fail-safe load path: two secondary HI-LOK pins on the bracket body.

Material: Inconel 718 per AMS 5663, precipitation hardened.

Design per CS-25.571 + FAR 25.571 + CS-E engine integration, including a CS-25.571 Amendment 18 fail-safe SF 1.0 (inspect-before-next-flight).

Load cases (placeholders pending patent fetch).
- LC1 forward thrust limit 45 kN along thrust axis.
- LC2 side load limit 15 kN (yaw gust).
- LC3 vertical (maneuver/gust) 12 kN.
- LC4 combined thrust + side at 0.75x each (33.75 + 11.25 kN).
- LC5 ultimate 1.5 x LC1 = 67.5 kN.
- LC6 fail-safe: 1.0x limit with the primary load path cut.

Additional gates: displacement at engine-side spherical bearing under LC1 <= 0.2 mm; fatigue life at 1e6 cycles of cyclic thrust (0 to 45 kN, Inconel 718 HCF fatigue limit ~700 MPa); mass reduction >= 25% vs solid-billet baseline.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: S-N curve + Miner's rule for fatigue life is a post-FEA closed-form step.
Redundant load path analysis is a structural scheme review, not FEA physics per se.

prompt.title: Inconel 718 engine thrust-link bracket with fail-safe path (engine-mount-bracket patent US9796464B2 style)
prompt.brief: Design a 280x180x120 mm Inconel 718 engine-mount bracket with redundant load path, passing CS-25.571 fail-safe + CS-25.305 limit/ultimate at 45 kN thrust limit.

boundary_conditions.pylon_interface: 8 bolts fully fixed (or 6 if primary path cut in LC6)

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: CS-25.571 fail-safe at 1.0x limit after primary-path cut
S-N curve Inconel 718 HCF limit ~700 MPa + Miner's rule

requirements.governing_standard: CS-25.305 + CS-25.571 + FAR 25.571 + AMS 5663 + CS-E engine integration

analysis_types: linear static limit + ultimate + fail-safe + fatigue post-proc

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Static limit/ultimate governs; engine-resonance is a separate brief.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Ambient up to engine nacelle; max case ~200 C is below Inconel 718 degradation.

notes.patent_fetch: Thrust/side/vertical limit loads are plausible engine-thrust-link placeholders; patent US9796464B2 text must be fetched for actual claim numbers.
notes.numeric_source: Inconel 718 AMS 5663 allowables; CS-25.571 fail-safe framework.
